content:
Hello! I am Edward Isayev, a 3rd year PhD student at Boston College. My research interests are in algebraic geometry, especially enumerative geometry, log geometry, mathematical physics and the many interactions therein. I’m very fortunate to be advised by Qile Chen.
Before BC, I was an undergraduate master’s (MMath) student at the University of Edinburgh, from which I graduated with First-Class Honours in July 2024. I am originally from Ireland.
Feel free to contact me about anything either mathematical or otherwise at surname@bc.edu. I’m always happy to chat!

title: About Me

content:
Here you can find some notes from various classes I’ve taken over the years, as well as some more expository notes on areas related to my thesis. Any mistakes made herein are entirely my own, and corrections would be greatly appreciated to my email.
Expository Notes # Gromov Witten Theory - Notes from a Spring 2026 course taught by Qile Chen.
